title: The Last Customer
premise: Ruth ran the corner store for thirty years; robbed twice and nearly out of everything, she comes to your barricade. What you spare — or don't — decides how she remembers you.
subject: npc.ruth

beats.plea: Ruth sways at your barricade, hollow-eyed and begging to be let in — she has nothing left. Sheltering her means sparing supplies from your cache.
beats.helped: Ruth is resting under your roof, some colour coming back to her.
beats.refused: Ruth is out there in the dark, and she knows exactly what your cache holds.
beats.resolvedGood: Ruth slipped back at first light and left supplies in your cache — she remembered.
beats.resolvedCold: Your cache has been torn into and the barricade wrenched loose — Ruth came back for what you would not give.

help.label: Take Ruth in — share the cache

notes: First authored arc (T40 · FR-STORY-01). The engine (src/sim/story.ts, THE_LAST_CUSTOMER) holds the authoritative VS dials; these mirror them for review and carry the surfaced prose. Ruth's desperate disposition (npc.ruth) starts her needs high so the trigger is reachable in a real run.
